title: How to Play ZXSpace
seoTitle: How to Play ZXSpace | BarkinMad Studios
description: Learn the ZXSpace session loop, city-defence objective, alien waves, shields, lives, scoring, power-ups, and wave rewards.
summary: ZXSpace is built around defending the city, clearing alien formations, protecting shields, and improving score across waves.

sections.heading: Goal
paragraphs: Move the cannon along the bottom of the playfield, shoot the alien formation, and defend the city below the shields.
A run ends when the ship is destroyed with no lives remaining or the city is destroyed.

sections.heading: Session flow
paragraphs: A normal session starts from the menu, enters play, spawns an alien formation, and continues until the wave is cleared or the player loses.
Clearing a wave advances to the next formation and awards coins through the shared ZX Series wallet flow.
bullets: Drag the cannon to aim horizontally
Let auto-fire keep pressure on the formation
Use shields and city space carefully as the aliens descend
Watch for UFO bonus targets and power-up drops

sections.heading: Scoring and waves
paragraphs: Alien rows have different score values, with top, middle, and bottom enemies awarding different points.
UFO targets award score only, while wave completion awards BarkinMad Coins through the app wallet.

sections.heading: Power-ups
paragraphs: Gameplay includes Double Laser and Shield Repair drops.
Double Laser temporarily fires two shots, while Shield Repair restores damaged shield protection when repairable segments exist.

featureShowcases.heading: Start playing ZXSpace
featureShowcases.summary: Learn the core ZXSpace session flow: defend the city, clear alien waves, preserve shields, and use power-ups at the right moment.
body: This gives new players enough context to start a session confidently and understand what the game is measuring.
Use this page first, then move into controls, game modes, and strategy when you want to improve consistency.
bullets: Learn the flow from setup into play.
Learn how controls, modes, and progress tracking.
Keeps beginner guidance practical and player-facing.

image.caption: ZXSpace how-to-play artwork shows the player flow into gameplay.
